mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates signal deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth reflects robust corporate activity that boosts earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 over any 1-year window, or 5-day forward returns in high INDPRO growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and inflation signals strong demand pressures that push central banks to tighten policy, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL breaks down during the period.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles characterized by rising federal funds rates and declining money supply growth create headwinds for equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or RSAFS growth diverges from FEDFUNDS without corresponding equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth indicates robust hiring that supports consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 180-day window, or 20-day forward returns in high PAYEMS growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts signal increased construction activity and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or HOUST growth fails to correlate with equity gains over 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates strong economic momentum that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or the joint RSAFS-UNRATE condition decouples from equity performance.
